prompt: I need an 8-week content calendar for our new B2B SaaS product launch -- we're releasing an AI-powered contract management platform called ClauseIQ targeted at mid-market legal ops teams (200-1000 emp...
assertions: Output includes the calendar in the specified table format with all required columns: Week, Date, Channel, Content Title, Format, Persona, CTA, and Status -- plus per-week sections for Key Theme, Pillar Link, and Production Deadline

The pillar-cluster model is clearly visible with at least 2 cornerstone assets (e.g. a pre-launch guide on AI contract management and a post-launch ROI report or case study framework) each generating 4-8 derived cluster pieces across blog, LinkedIn, and email

Channel cadence stays within sustainable defaults for the four committed channels only: LinkedIn company page at up to 3x/week, CEO LinkedIn at up to 4-5x/week, blog at 1-2x/week, email at 1-2x/month -- with NO webinar or paid social content planned since those channels are explicitly uncommitted

Total weekly content volume does not exceed 6-9 pieces per week (1 full-time writer at 3-4 pieces/week with 2-3x AI multiplier), and if any week approaches or exceeds this ceiling the output flags it and recommends what to cut, prioritising pillar content over channel fill

Every production deadline is set at least 5 business days before the corresponding publish date, and the LegalTech Connect event in week 3 has content (pre-event teasers, live coverage, post-event recap) with deadlines that account for the event timeline

Every single content piece is anchored to one of the two personas (VP Legal Ops or Head of Procurement) and includes a specific CTA (e.g. 'Book a demo', 'Download the guide', 'Register for launch event') -- no piece exists without both a persona and a CTA
